questions.q: In which city is the Gateway of India located?
questions.explanation: The Gateway of India stands on the waterfront at Apollo Bunder in Mumbai (formerly Bombay).

questions.q: What is the overall architectural form of the Gateway of India?
questions.explanation: The Gateway of India is a large triumphal arch built overlooking the Arabian Sea.

questions.q: In which Indian state is the Gateway of India situated?
questions.explanation: Mumbai, where the Gateway of India is located, is the capital of the state of Maharashtra.

questions.q: The Gateway of India overlooks which body of water?
questions.explanation: The Gateway of India faces the harbour on the Arabian Sea along Mumbai's southern waterfront.

questions.q: In which year was the Gateway of India completed?
questions.explanation: The monument was completed in 1924, although its foundation had been laid years earlier.

questions.q: During which period of Indian history was the Gateway of India built?
questions.explanation: The Gateway of India was erected during the British colonial period in the early 20th century.

questions.q: The Gateway of India is famous as the point through which the last British troops did what in 1948?
questions.explanation: In 1948 the last British troops departed independent India by sea through the Gateway of India.

questions.q: A famous luxury hotel stands directly opposite the Gateway of India. Which one?
questions.explanation: The Taj Mahal Palace Hotel sits across from the Gateway of India and shares the same iconic Mumbai waterfront.

questions.q: What is the main building material of the Gateway of India?
questions.explanation: The Gateway of India is built largely of yellow Kharodi basalt stone, a local volcanic rock.

questions.q: The Gateway of India is best described as which kind of landmark?
questions.explanation: Today the Gateway of India is one of Mumbai's most visited monuments and a major tourist attraction.

questions.q: The Gateway of India was originally built to commemorate the 1911 visit of which royal couple?
options: King George V and Queen Mary
King Edward VII and Queen Alexandra
King George VI and Queen Elizabeth
questions.explanation: It was built to commemorate the landing of King-Emperor George V and Queen Mary at Bombay in December 1911 for the Delhi Durbar.

questions.q: Who was the British architect who designed the Gateway of India?
questions.explanation: The Scottish architect George Wittet designed the Gateway of India, completing it in 1924.

questions.q: The Gateway of India's design is primarily based on which architectural style?
questions.explanation: George Wittet designed it in the Indo-Saracenic style, incorporating elements of 16th-century Gujarati architecture.

questions.q: Approximately how tall is the central arch of the Gateway of India?
questions.explanation: The Gateway of India rises to a height of about 26 metres (roughly 85 feet).

questions.q: In which Mumbai locality is the Gateway of India situated?
questions.explanation: The Gateway of India stands at Apollo Bunder in the Colaba area of South Mumbai.

questions.q: Before the final stone monument was built, what marked the spot for the 1911 royal visit?
questions.explanation: Only a cardboard model of the gateway was ready when King George V visited in 1911; the permanent stone structure came later.

questions.q: In what year was the foundation stone of the Gateway of India laid?
questions.explanation: The foundation stone was laid in 1913, and the monument was finally completed in 1924.

questions.q: An equestrian statue of which Maratha king now stands near the Gateway of India?
questions.explanation: A statue of the Maratha founder Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj was later installed in the plaza beside the Gateway of India.

questions.q: From the Gateway of India, ferries regularly take visitors to which nearby UNESCO World Heritage site?
questions.explanation: Boats depart from the Gateway of India to Elephanta Island, home of the rock-cut Elephanta Caves.

questions.q: Which statesman is associated with another statue placed at the Gateway of India site?
questions.explanation: A statue of Swami Vivekananda was also installed near the Gateway of India alongside that of Chhatrapati Shivaji.

questions.q: What was Mumbai called at the time the Gateway of India was built?
questions.explanation: The city was officially known as Bombay until it was renamed Mumbai in 1995.

questions.q: How many turrets crown the top of the Gateway of India?
questions.explanation: The monument is topped by four turrets, with the large central dome dominating the structure.

questions.q: What is the approximate diameter of the central dome of the Gateway of India?
questions.explanation: The large central dome is about 15 metres (roughly 48 feet) in diameter.

questions.q: The Gateway of India's foundation stone was laid by which colonial official in 1913?
options: Sir George Sydenham Clarke, Governor of Bombay
questions.explanation: The foundation stone was laid in 1913 by Sir George Sydenham Clarke, then Governor of Bombay.

questions.q: The last British regiment to leave India through the Gateway in 1948 was associated with which famous group?
options: The First Battalion of the Somerset Light Infantry
questions.explanation: The First Battalion of the Somerset Light Infantry was among the last British troops to depart India through the Gateway in February 1948.

questions.q: George Wittet, designer of the Gateway, also designed which other major Mumbai institution?
options: The Prince of Wales Museum (now Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Vastu Sangrahalaya)
questions.explanation: George Wittet also designed the Prince of Wales Museum, now the Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Vastu Sangrahalaya, in the same Indo-Saracenic style.

questions.q: The decorative latticework and details of the Gateway draw especially on the architecture of which Indian region?
questions.explanation: Wittet drew on 16th-century Gujarati architectural motifs, giving the arch its Indo-Saracenic character.

questions.q: The Gateway of India's central arch was intended to function symbolically as what for arriving dignitaries?
options: A ceremonial entry point to India by sea
questions.explanation: It was conceived as a grand ceremonial gateway through which viceroys and important visitors would first set foot in India by sea.

questions.q: Roughly how long did construction of the Gateway take from foundation stone to completion?
questions.explanation: Construction spanned roughly 11 years, from the laying of the foundation stone in 1913 to completion in 1924.

questions.q: What was a major reason the road behind the Gateway, meant to lead inland, was never fully built as planned?
options: Lack of funds, so the grand approach avenue was never completed
A change of capital to Delhi
questions.explanation: The planned grand approach road leading from the Gateway into the city was never fully realised, largely due to funding limitations.
